name: 12306 MCP
what_it_does: An MCP server that enables AI models to search Chinese railway (12306) train tickets, query station stops, filter train info, and find multi-leg transfer routes.
use_cases: Searching available train tickets between Chinese cities via AI assistant
Finding optimal transfer routes for multi-leg train journeys
Querying stop-by-stop station information for a specific train
not_for: Booking or purchasing train tickets (search only)
Non-Chinese railway systems
Real-time train tracking or delay information
best_when: An agent needs to search Chinese railway ticket availability or plan train routes within China.
avoid_when: You need to book tickets, handle payments, or search non-Chinese rail systems.

auth.notes: No authentication required. Queries the public 12306 API.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors are primarily returned from the 12306 upstream; sparse local error handling
known_agent_gotchas: Documentation and tool descriptions are primarily in Chinese - agents may struggle with non-Chinese language contexts
Dependent on 12306 upstream availability which can be unreliable during peak travel seasons
Station names must use Chinese characters or station codes
No ticket purchasing capability - search only

security_score_components.security_notes: No secrets required; open source tool that queries public 12306 API. TLS depends on 12306 upstream enforcement. Minimal attack surface as read-only MCP.

name: A2V: AI Value Compute Protocol
what_it_does: A distributed protocol that claims to enable AI agents to measure, exchange, and settle value on-chain, positioning itself as payment infrastructure for the AI economy.
use_cases: On-chain settlement of AI agent-to-agent transactions
Metering and billing AI model API calls via smart contracts
Registering AI agents in a decentralized value network
not_for: Actual MCP tool integration (no real MCP server implementation found)
Production AI agent workflows requiring reliable tooling
Users who need concrete, working software rather than protocol vision
best_when: You are building blockchain-based AI agent payment infrastructure and want to explore an early-stage protocol concept.
avoid_when: You need a working MCP server or concrete tooling - this repo is primarily a marketing website with aspirational claims about MCP compatibility.

auth.notes: Requires Web3 wallet (MetaMask, WalletConnect) for blockchain transactions. No traditional API auth.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No real API to generate errors from - mostly aspirational documentation
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Blockchain transactions are not easily reversible; idempotency not documented
known_agent_gotchas: Despite claiming MCP compatibility, no actual MCP server implementation exists in the repository
Repository is primarily a marketing/documentation website (Next.js frontend), not functional tooling
Claims about 10,000+ MCP tools are aspirational, not implemented
Web3 wallet requirement creates significant friction for AI agent integration
High star count may be inflated relative to actual functionality

security_score_components.security_notes: Web3 wallet auth is unconventional for API integrations. Smart contracts are publicly auditable but the protocol is too immature to assess properly. Private key management risk is significant.

name: ABCoder
what_it_does: An AI-oriented code-processing framework that parses source code into a universal AST format and exposes it via MCP tools, enabling LLMs to have deep, structured, and confidential understanding of codebases without uploading code.
use_cases: Providing structured codebase context to AI coding assistants via MCP
Analyzing large codebases locally without sending code to external services
Generating code task specifications with full dependency awareness across Go, Rust, Python, JS/TS, Java, and C
not_for: Runtime code execution or debugging
Projects in languages without parser support
Simple scripts where full AST analysis is overkill
best_when: An AI coding agent needs deep, structured understanding of a codebase's symbols, dependencies, and architecture without uploading code externally.
avoid_when: You need runtime debugging, or your project is in a language not yet supported by the parser.

auth.notes: No auth for local MCP server usage. Optional API key needed only if using the agent mode with external LLM providers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error messages are functional but not always actionable - standard Go CLI error output
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All operations are read-only analysis; fully idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Go writer is fully supported but other language writers are still 'Coming Soon'
Requires pre-parsing step (abcoder parse) before MCP server can serve data
AST directory must be pre-generated and passed as argument to MCP server
Some documentation is in Chinese with English translation available
Backed by CloudWeGo (ByteDance) - active maintenance expected

security_score_components.security_notes: Entirely local tool - no network exposure for core functionality. Excellent security posture for a local analysis tool. Optional LLM integration uses API keys via env vars.

name: Abstract API
what_it_does: Suite of simple REST APIs covering email validation, phone validation, IP geolocation, VAT validation, and other utility data lookups, designed for developer convenience with consistent interfaces.
use_cases: Email validation at signup without integrating a dedicated email validation service
Phone number validation and formatting for international phone numbers
IP geolocation to determine user's country/region for localization or fraud detection
VAT number validation for European B2B invoicing workflows
Exchange rate lookup for multi-currency pricing in e-commerce agents
not_for: High-volume email validation at scale (use ZeroBounce or NeverBounce for bulk lists)
Deep email deliverability scoring with sub-status codes
Enterprise-grade data quality with SLAs
Applications needing more than one specific API product covered by Abstract
best_when: You need several simple validation APIs in one place with a consistent interface, low integration overhead, and acceptable accuracy for form-level validation.
avoid_when: You need bulk validation, high accuracy for mission-critical email deliverability, or specialized data beyond what Abstract's basic APIs cover.

auth.notes: Separate API key per product (email, phone, IP geolocation, etc.). Each product has its own free tier and rate limits. Keys as query parameters.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes with JSON error details; adequate for agent consumption
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All lookup endpoints are read-only and idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: Separate API keys per product - agents using multiple Abstract APIs need to manage multiple keys
Free tier limits (100-1,000/month) can be hit quickly in testing
Email validation accuracy is basic compared to dedicated services like ZeroBounce
No batch endpoint - all validations are one-at-a-time synchronous calls
Rate limits are monthly caps, not per-minute - can exhaust entire month's quota quickly
No SDK - agents must implement HTTP requests directly

security_score_components.security_notes: API keys passed as query parameters (not headers) is a mild security concern - keys can appear in server logs and browser history. HTTPS enforced. GDPR compliant.

name: AbuseIPDB API
what_it_does: Crowdsourced IP address reputation database for checking and reporting abusive IPs. Aggregates abuse reports from thousands of contributors for spam, DDoS, brute force, and malicious activity.
use_cases: Checking IP reputation before allowing access in security-aware agent workflows
Enriching security incidents with IP abuse history and confidence scores
Automated firewall rule generation based on IP abuse scores
Reporting abusive IPs back to the community database
Blocking known malicious IPs in web application security layers
not_for: File or URL malware scanning (use VirusTotal instead)
Domain reputation (limited to IP addresses)
Definitive blocking - crowdsourced data has false positives
High-frequency real-time blocking at network speeds
best_when: An agent needs fast, simple IP reputation scoring to filter out known-bad actors, especially for abuse categories like spam, scanning, or brute force.
avoid_when: You need file or URL scanning, domain reputation, or very high throughput real-time checks.

auth.notes: API key passed via Key header. Simple single-key model. Free and paid tiers use same authentication mechanism.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard HTTP error codes with JSON body; rate limit errors clearly communicated
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Check endpoint is read-only. Report endpoint is not idempotent but has cooldown period.
known_agent_gotchas: Confidence score of 100 doesn't mean definitely malicious - just high community report volume
Private IPs (RFC1918) return errors - agents must filter these before querying
Daily limits reset at midnight UTC - agents need to track usage across calls
Bulk endpoint (CIDR ranges) only available on paid tiers
Report endpoint requires specifying abuse categories from a fixed enum - agents must know the category codes

security_score_components.security_notes: Security-focused service with HTTPS enforced. API key via header (good practice). GDPR compliant. No scope granularity - one key has full read/write access to the account.

name: ActiveCampaign API
what_it_does: ActiveCampaign's REST API for email marketing automation, CRM, contact management, deal pipelines, and customer journey automation for SMBs.
use_cases: Adding and updating contacts with custom field data
Triggering automation sequences based on external events
Managing deals and pipelines in the built-in CRM
Sending targeted emails to segmented lists
Tracking contact engagement and automation enrollment
not_for: Enterprise marketing at Marketo/SFMC scale
Large-volume transactional email (use SendGrid or Mailgun)
Advanced B2B account-based marketing
Real-time push notifications
best_when: SMBs need integrated email marketing + CRM automation without the complexity of enterprise platforms.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade compliance, very large contact lists, or deep enterprise CRM integration.

auth.notes: API key passed in request headers (Api-Token). Account-specific base URL required. No OAuth2 - single API key per account with full access.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with field-level validation messages; decent for agent consumption
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Contact creation is idempotent by email. Other operations are not.
known_agent_gotchas: Account-specific base URL means hardcoded URLs won't work - fetch from account settings or config
Contact creation returns existing contact if email matches - useful for agent deduplication
API key has full account access - no scoping possible; treat as high-privilege credential
Rate limits are low (5 req/s) - agents hitting bulk contact operations need careful throttling
Custom fields require fetching field IDs first - no name-based access
Webhooks are per-event type - need separate webhook URLs for different event types

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key with full account access is a security risk - no scope granularity. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Account-specific base URL requires careful configuration.

name: Activepieces
what_it_does: An open-source workflow automation platform (Zapier alternative) with 280+ integrations, a visual no-code builder, AI agent capabilities, and MCP server support that exposes all pieces as MCP-compatible tools.
use_cases: Building automated workflows connecting 280+ services without code
Exposing workflow automation pieces as MCP tools for AI agents
Creating human-in-the-loop approval workflows with AI agent integration
not_for: Simple one-off API calls that don't need workflow orchestration
Real-time sub-second event processing
Scenarios requiring zero infrastructure (requires self-hosting or cloud instance)
best_when: An agent needs to orchestrate multi-step workflows across many services, or you want to expose 280+ integration pieces as MCP tools.
avoid_when: You only need a single integration point, or you need sub-second latency for real-time processing.

auth.notes: Self-hosted instance requires configuration. Individual pieces may require their own OAuth or API key auth to connected services.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Workflow execution errors are surfaced at the step level; MCP error messages less documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Depends on individual piece implementation; workflow engine doesn't guarantee idempotency
known_agent_gotchas: Requires running a full Activepieces instance (Docker) before MCP tools are available
280+ pieces as MCP tools could overwhelm agent tool selection - consider filtering
Individual piece auth (OAuth for Google, Slack, etc.) must be configured per-connection
MCP server is a newer feature - may not be as battle-tested as core workflow engine
Self-hosted deployment adds operational complexity

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted means TLS is operator's responsibility - not enforced by default. Individual piece OAuth flows provide reasonable auth. Credentials encrypted in DB but encryption strength depends on deployment.

name: Aderyn
what_it_does: A Rust-based static analyzer built specifically for Solidity smart contracts by Cyfrin. Analyzes AST of Solidity contracts to detect vulnerability patterns including reentrancy, unchecked return values, weak randomness, and centralization risks. Supports Foundry and Hardhat project layouts with zero configuration, outputting reports in Markdown, JSON, or SARIF.
use_cases: Automated pre-deployment security scanning of Solidity contracts in CI/CD pipelines
Generating SARIF reports for GitHub Code Scanning integration
AI-assisted smart contract review: agent runs Aderyn, interprets findings, and proposes fixes
Security audit preparation: pre-screen contracts before a full manual audit
DeFi protocol monitoring via GitHub Actions
not_for: Non-Solidity smart contract languages (Vyper, Rust/Anchor, Move, Cairo)
Runtime monitoring or dynamic analysis of deployed on-chain contracts
Full security audits - Aderyn catches known patterns but misses novel vulnerabilities
Ethereum mainnet live contract bytecode analysis (requires source and compilation)
best_when: An agent or CI/CD pipeline needs fast, automated detection of known Solidity vulnerability patterns before deployment.
avoid_when: You are working with non-Solidity contracts, need dynamic/runtime analysis, or require comprehensive audit coverage.

auth.notes: Runs entirely locally as a CLI tool and MCP server. No authentication, API keys, or network calls required.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI errors are clear; MCP interface errors less documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Static analysis is read-only and fully idempotent
known_agent_gotchas: MCP server capability is GitHub-tagged but not documented - no published tool definitions or schemas
Requires complete Foundry or Hardhat project structure - won't analyze standalone .sol files
Windows requires WSL
GPL-3.0 copyleft license: linking into larger system may require GPL-3.0
Analysis time scales with codebase size - large DeFi protocols may exceed MCP timeouts
False positives for centralization risk detectors - agents should flag for human review

security_score_components.security_notes: Local CLI tool with excellent security posture - no network exposure, no secrets required. Rust implementation reduces memory-safety vulnerabilities. Maintained by professional security audit firm.

name: Adobe Acrobat Sign API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade e-signature platform API for sending, tracking, and managing legally binding digital signature workflows at scale, with deep integration into Adobe Document Cloud.
use_cases: Sending contracts and agreements for electronic signature from automated workflows
Building custom signature flows with bulk send capabilities
Integrating e-signature into enterprise CRM or ERP systems
Tracking agreement status (sent, viewed, signed, completed) programmatically
Managing signature delegation, reminders, and expiration workflows
not_for: Simple PDF generation without signing requirements
Startups or SMBs (Adobe Sign is expensive for low volume)
Consumer-facing signature UX (DocuSign and PandaDoc have better consumer UX)
best_when: You're in an enterprise environment with existing Adobe Document Cloud licensing and need compliant e-signature workflows with strict legal requirements.
avoid_when: You need a cost-effective signature solution for low-to-medium volume - PandaDoc or HelloSign are more accessible.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with access tokens and refresh tokens. Scoped permissions (agreement_read, agreement_send, user_read, etc.). Service accounts use OAuth2 client credentials flow.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard REST error codes with error descriptions; datacenter region errors can be confusing
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Agreement creation is not idempotent by default; requires explicit deduplication logic
known_agent_gotchas: Adobe Sign has multiple datacenter regions - API endpoint must match account region
Access tokens expire after 3600 seconds - agents must implement token refresh
Transient document upload is required before creating agreements - two-step process
Agreement status polling required - webhook setup is complex
OAuth app requires approval for production use - separate client IDs for sandbox/prod

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise-grade security with OAuth2 scopes, HIPAA, eIDAS, and ESIGN compliance. Multiple datacenter regions for data residency. Token expiry and refresh properly implemented.

name: Adyen API
what_it_does: Enterprise global payment platform providing end-to-end acquiring, payment processing, risk management, and financial services for large-scale merchants across online, in-store, and mobile channels.
use_cases: Processing global payments with unified acquiring across 150+ currencies
Marketplace and platform payment splits using Adyen for Platforms
Agent-driven payment authorization, capture, and refund workflows
Risk and fraud scoring via RevenueProtect as part of payment pipelines
Unified commerce integrations linking online and in-store payment data
not_for: Small businesses or startups (requires substantial transaction volume and enterprise contract)
Developers who need a simple sandbox to prototype quickly (onboarding takes weeks)
Micropayments or very low-volume merchants
best_when: You're a large-scale merchant or marketplace processing high payment volumes globally and need a single payment platform.
avoid_when: You're a small business, startup, or low-volume merchant - Stripe or Square will be far easier to work with.

auth.notes: API key for server-side calls (X-API-Key header). Client key for client-side Drop-in and Components. Role-based API credentials managed in the Customer Area.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent structured error responses with refusalReason, resultCode, and additional data
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Payment reference must be unique per transaction; duplicate references return original result
known_agent_gotchas: Account onboarding requires manual approval - no self-serve sandbox
Webhook signatures must be validated using HMAC-SHA256
Payment reference must be unique per transaction
Separate APIs per product area (Checkout, Management, Reporting)
LIVE environment requires separate API credentials and different endpoint URLs
Currency amounts are in minor units (cents)

security_score_components.security_notes: PCI-DSS Level 1 certified. API keys via header, HMAC-SHA256 webhook validation. Role-based credentials in Customer Area. Excellent security posture for a payment processor.
